subject: Statistics
title: The Elo Rating System
description: A fast breakdown of The Elo Rating System for builders.

hook.text: How do you rank chess players who never play each other? Arpad Elo solved it in 1960.

buildup.text: Elo assigns a number to each player. Win against a higher-rated opponent and gain more points.

discovery.text: The difference in ratings predicts the probability of winning. 200 points higher means about 75% win chance.

twist.text: Elo was designed for chess but now rates tennis, football, video games, and even dating apps.

climax.text: Its elegance is in the update rule: gain points proportional to how surprising the result was.

punchline.text: Beat someone better and your rating soars. One formula rules all.

quiz.question: How does the Elo rating system assign point changes?
options: You gain more points for beating higher-rated opponents — points reflect how surprising the result was
Every win gives the same number of points
